instruction: Confident, calm hackathon narration. Native English pronunciation for MirrorRun, Crusoe, Nemotron, TrueFoundry, Perfect Corp, and Lark. Speak at about 150 words per minute.

chapters.text: A try-on demo can look polished and still fail the first shopper on launch day. MirrorRun records the launch path and the recovery proof in one receipt.
The usual choices split the story. The shopper sees an image, operations sees a dashboard, and QA sees a chat thread. MirrorRun keeps the image, trace, fault, and replay together.
Here is the launch room. We create the session, open the mobile path, run the sponsor adapters, and keep every result or blocker visible.
Perfect Corp owns the consumer result. Crusoe and Nemotron write the launch plan. TrueFoundry records resilience behavior. Lark turns the session into a replay workflow.
Inspect the receipt, try the phone path, and use the same run to replace blockers with real provider evidence after the keys are installed.
